title: astrocasia peltata
intro: Astrocasia peltata, also known as Indian long pepper or pippali, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. It is a climber with heart-shaped leaves and small, yellowish-green flowers, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. Astrocasia peltata is valued for its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is commonly used in the form of powder, oil, or decoction, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
